ticket.subject: Presale questions
ticket.description:
Hi, I have a couple of questions about your plugins. I'm building a website for a client who uses TNT for shipping but also use Australia Post as well as FedEx for international shipping. Firstly is there a way to bundle products or do i have to purchase them individually. Secondly TNT applies an additional fuel surcharge, which is calculated as a percentage of the shipping cost. This percentage can change from month to month.

My client wants to pass this extra cost on to their customers and i need to know if there is an option in the plugin to do this or if the surchage is added into the shipping when it doesplays on the website.

For example:
If the shipping cost is $20 and the fuel surcharge is 10%, the final shipping total would be $22. If the surcharge changes to 12% the next month, the total would then be $22.40.

Greetings from PluginHive!

Thank you for your interest in our PH Australia Post MyPost Business Shipping for WooCommerce Plugin.I would like to inform you that our plugin fetches live shipping rates directly from the Australia Post API, and these rates typically include applicable surcharges. You can also automate label generation and tracking using the plugin.

In addition, the plugin provides an option to add a custom handling fee to the shipping rates returned by the API, which can help you account for any extra charges such as fuel surcharges.

Thank you for reaching out with your query.

The packaging setup is needed in the plugin so that the plugin can request rates based on actual package sizes and weights rather than just the weight of the products.

You can refer to the following plugin documentation to take assistance in the plugin setup - https://www.pluginhive.com/knowledge-base/setting-up-woocommerce-shipping-services/

If needed, we can also setup a Zoom call to discuss your requirements and assist you with the plugin setup on your store. You can **CLICK HERE** to schedule the call as per your availability.

Looking forward to your response.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

Call with developer of the store. Customer added TNT Au and MPB for rates on checkout.
Labels are not needed as the store owner uses other source for it.

Updated the same and synced the details in the plugin. Post this the rates were available on checkout.
Discussed further requirements about packaging and also how to adjust the rates on checkout using the rules in the plugin.
Review requested.

comments.body:
Hi Sagar,



Some of these products needed to be done different in terms of their weight as they have additional fields to can adjust the size of them. For example most of these products are measured in meters and you can add 5m, 10m and 15m. You can also add two 5m three 10m and four 15m and the weights are going to be different for each so I have had to add the weights to the individual options so for example for one product under the 50m length I have added 0.04kg. when this is add it will times it by 50 (used as quantity) which will equal 2kg which is what the product actually weighs.



Hopefully this makes sense.





Kind regards,

Hello Jarrod,

Thank you for your patience.

We have increased the maximum product quantity limit to 3000 for your store, so higher quantity orders should now calculate rates properly.

To ensure everything works correctly with your variation weights, please check the following:
1. Make sure each product variation has a weight added in WooCommerce
2. After confirming, run a product sync/import in the WSS app so it updates the latest weight data.
3. Test again with a higher quantity product (for example, 50m × quantity 50) to see if rates are returned correctly.

If you still do not see rates after this, please share the product and quantity you tested so we can look into it further.

Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Jarrod Clark,

In Plugin > Settings > Shipping > Packaging, you have selected the Weight Based Packing method. In this case, the shipping rates are calculated only based on the total weight of the order, and product dimensions are not considered.

Could you please try calculating the rates using the Australia Post rate calculator based on the total weight and cross-check once?

If you would like the items to be packed into boxes and calculate rates based on both weight and dimensions, please update the packaging method to Box Packing and configure the required box dimensions.

Please note that with the Box Packing method, the items will be packed into the boxes you have added based on the total weight and volume of the products.

Regarding the shipping rate issue for certain postal codes, could you please share a screenshot from the checkout page where the shipping price is not showing? This will help us investigate further.

Please let us know how it goes or if you need any assistance from our end.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

Hello Jarrod,

Thank you for your time over the call.

Regarding the packaging, for a product to get packed correctly in a box added in the plugin, the dimensions to the product has to be given correctly.

Taking an example: For the product "Custom Promotional Bunting" to get packed in the box named 52x36x35 which can accomodate upto 500 quantities of this product, you can give the dimensions to this product as 52x36x0.07 cm. Here 0.07 cm is calculated using the formula "Actual Height of the Product / Max Qty box can hold" which is 35/500

Regarding the rates case, we will check further with our team and get back to you.

Best regards,
Sagar K S
PluginHive

Taking an example: For the product "Custom Promotional Bunting" to get packed in the box named 52x36x35 which can accomodate upto 500 quantities of this product, you can give the dimensions to this product as 52x36x0.07 cm. Here 0.07 cm is calculated using the formula "Actual Height of the Product / Max Qty box can hold" which is 35/500
 
Regarding the rates case, we will check further with our team and get back to you.
 
Best regards,
Sagar K S
PluginHive
